name: Chocolate Fondant
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Squares Unsweetened Chocolate, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size deep saucepan, mix sugar, corn syrup, and water. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring to dissolve sugar.
2. When boiling, wash sugar crystals from the side of the pan with a pastry brush dipped in water.
3. Set candy thermometer into the pan and bring syrup to 242°F (firm ball stage).
4. Rinse a large platter in cold water but do not dry it.
5. Pour syrup onto the platter but do not scrape the pan. Let stand 5 minutes or until the surface feels just warm, moving the platter a few times to cool the surface.
6. Work candy with a spatula or wooden spoon, scraping to the center of the platter until white and firm.
7. Scrape into a heavy or doubled plastic bag. Add melted unsweetened chocolate. Close the bag and knead until candy is well mixed, smooth, and clings together.
8. Shape into 1-inch balls. Press pecan halves into half the balls.
9. Coat remaining balls: Melt white chocolate in a small saucepan over very low heat, stirring occasionally (do not overheat; chocolate will separate).
10. Spear fondant balls on fork and dip into white chocolate. Tap fork against rim of pan to knock off excess chocolate.
11. Place balls on waxed paper to cool. Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place. Keeps about 1 month.
